tasks.name: Phase 1: Complete Organizer Review Interface
tasks.description: This phase focuses on implementing the critical missing components for organizers to review and manage submissions. This is the highest priority gap in the current system as it blocks the complete submission workflow.
tasks.notes: This phase builds on the existing event management dashboard but adds specialized submission review capabilities.

relatedFiles.description: Existing event management page to understand patterns and extend
Existing submission actions to leverage

tasks.implementationGuide: Follow existing UI patterns with ProfileCard, form components, and action buttons. Ensure all components support RTL layouts for Arabic and maintain the existing security model checking subscriptions and user roles.
tasks.verificationCriteria: The organizer review interface should allow viewing all submissions for an event, filtering by status, viewing submission details, and providing feedback with status updates. It should maintain security by checking user roles and event ownership.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the Events_and_Submissions_Flow_Tasks.md analysis, the Eventy360 platform needs a comprehensive implementation plan to complete the Events and Submissions features. The current implementation has strong backend database functions but incomplete UI components, particularly for the organizer review process. The system architecture is well-defined with server-side functions in Supabase, Next.js page components organized by locale and user role, shared UI components, strong Arabic internationalization with partial English/French support, and route-based authentication and role checking.

tasks.name: Task 1.1: Create Abstract Review UI Component
tasks.description: Develop a reusable component for organizers to review abstract submissions. The component should display abstract content, allow for feedback entry, and enable status updates (accept/reject).
tasks.notes: This component is the highest priority as it enables the core review workflow. It should follow the existing pattern of multilingual form inputs as seen in the SubmissionForm component.

relatedFiles.description: Existing submission form to understand multilingual input patterns

tasks.verificationCriteria: Component should display submission details correctly, allow multilingual feedback entry, trigger the review_abstract() function when status is updated, and handle loading/error states appropriately.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the Events_and_Submissions_Flow_Tasks.md analysis, the Eventy360 platform needs a comprehensive implementation plan to complete the Events and Submissions features. The current implementation has strong backend database functions but incomplete UI components, particularly for the organizer review process. The system architecture is well-defined with server-side functions in Supabase, Next.js page components organized by locale and user role, shared UI components, strong Arabic internationalization with partial English/French support, and route-based authentication and role checking.
tasks.summary: Created a comprehensive AbstractReviewComponent that displays submission details, enables multilingual feedback entry via language tabs, and provides accept/reject functionality using the review_abstract database function. The component properly handles loading/error states and implements proper TypeScript types for all data structures.

tasks.name: Task 1.2: Create Full Paper Review UI Component
tasks.description: Develop a reusable component for organizers to review full paper submissions. The component should display paper details, allow file download, enable feedback entry, and provide status update options (accept/reject/request revision).
tasks.notes: This component builds on the abstract review component but adds file handling and additional status options.

relatedFiles.description: Existing submission form to understand file handling patterns

tasks.verificationCriteria: Component should display submission details correctly, allow file download, enable multilingual feedback entry, trigger the review_full_paper() function when status is updated, and handle loading/error states appropriately.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the Events_and_Submissions_Flow_Tasks.md analysis, the Eventy360 platform needs a comprehensive implementation plan to complete the Events and Submissions features. The current implementation has strong backend database functions but incomplete UI components, particularly for the organizer review process. The system architecture is well-defined with server-side functions in Supabase, Next.js page components organized by locale and user role, shared UI components, strong Arabic internationalization with partial English/French support, and route-based authentication and role checking.
tasks.summary: Created a comprehensive FullPaperReviewComponent that displays submission details, enables file download, allows multilingual feedback entry via language tabs, and provides three status update options (accept/reject/request revision) using the review_full_paper database function. The component properly handles loading/error states, properly implements TypeScript types, and includes a visual status indicator.

tasks.name: Task 1.3: Enhance Organizer Submissions Management Interface
tasks.description: Improve the existing event management dashboard to include a more robust section for managing submissions. This interface should allow organizers to view, filter, and take actions on submissions to their events.
tasks.notes: This task corresponds to the existing task ID `899f8b50-0558-4935-ad8a-9e33b742c7e9` which is already pending in the task system. This implementation should maintain that task ID.

relatedFiles.description: Existing event management page to extend with submissions tab

tasks.verificationCriteria: Interface should display all submissions for the event, allow filtering by status and other criteria, provide action buttons for review, and maintain security by verifying organizer ownership of the event.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the Events_and_Submissions_Flow_Tasks.md analysis, the Eventy360 platform needs a comprehensive implementation plan to complete the Events and Submissions features. The current implementation has strong backend database functions but incomplete UI components, particularly for the organizer review process. The system architecture is well-defined with server-side functions in Supabase, Next.js page components organized by locale and user role, shared UI components, strong Arabic internationalization with partial English/French support, and route-based authentication and role checking.
tasks.summary:
Completed the organizer submissions management interface enhancement with the following features:
1. Created a dedicated page for event organizers to view all submissions for their events
2. Implemented filters for submission status and text search capabilities
3. Added a responsive table view with pagination for better user experience
4. Displayed key submission details including title, researcher info, status, and update date
5. Added action links for reviewing different submission types based on their current status
6. Ensured proper security by verifying event ownership or admin status before access

tasks.name: Task 1.4: Create Submission Detail Page for Organizers
tasks.description: Develop a page for event organizers to view and manage detailed information about a specific submission. This page should allow organizers to update the submission status, provide feedback, and download attached files.
tasks.notes: This task corresponds to the existing task ID `1745d2d0-2a18-4c6a-9b15-5cf60ab4debc` which is already pending in the task system. This implementation should maintain that task ID.

relatedFiles.description: Researcher's submission detail page for reference

tasks.verificationCriteria: Page should display comprehensive submission details, embed the appropriate review component based on status, enforce security by verifying organizer ownership, and maintain consistent navigation with breadcrumbs or back links.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the Events_and_Submissions_Flow_Tasks.md analysis, the Eventy360 platform needs a comprehensive implementation plan to complete the Events and Submissions features. The current implementation has strong backend database functions but incomplete UI components, particularly for the organizer review process. The system architecture is well-defined with server-side functions in Supabase, Next.js page components organized by locale and user role, shared UI components, strong Arabic internationalization with partial English/French support, and route-based authentication and role checking.
tasks.summary:
Successfully implemented the submission detail page for event organizers, allowing them to view and manage submission details, including:
1. Created a detail page at `/[locale]/profile/events/[id]/submissions/[submissionId]` showing submission data
2. Implemented proper security checks to ensure only event owners or admins can access
3. Added a responsive UI showing submission title, abstract, keywords, and files
4. Displayed researcher information with contact details
5. Added conditional action buttons based on submission status
6. Created an abstract review form for organizers to approve/reject abstracts with feedback
7. Ensured proper RTL support for Arabic language and localization

tasks.implementationGuide:
Review all new components and identify missing translation keys. Update the translation files for all three languages. Ensure consistent terminology across languages.

Key areas to update:
1. Abstract and full paper review interfaces
2. Submission status messages and labels
3. Feedback forms and prompts
4. Error messages and validation text
5. Action button labels
6. Statistics and metrics labels
tasks.verificationCriteria: All user-facing text in the submission system should be properly internationalized in all three languages. No hardcoded strings should appear in the UI. RTL layout should be properly maintained for Arabic.

tasks.notes: Ensure the bookmarking functionality is only available to authenticated users with active subscriptions.

tasks.verificationCriteria: Users should be able to bookmark and unbookmark events, view a list of their bookmarked events, and receive appropriate feedback when bookmarking actions succeed or fail. Bookmarking should be restricted to users with active subscriptions.
